name: User Interface
description: How UI components are generated where data lives and streamed to browsers with two-way binding

content.title: User Interface Architecture
content.logo: data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg viewBox='0 0 48 48' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%3E%3Cdefs%3E%3ClinearGradient id='ui' x1='0%25' y1='0%25' x2='100%25' y2='100%25'%3E%3Cstop offset='0%25' stop-color='%239c27b0'/%3E%3Cstop offset='100%25' stop-color='%236a1b9a'/%3E%3C/linearGradient%3E%3C/defs%3E%3Crect width='48' height='48' rx='8' fill='url(%23ui)'/%3E%3Crect x='8' y='10' width='32' height='24' rx='2' fill='white'/%3E%3Crect x='10' y='12' width='12' height='8' rx='1' fill='%239c27b0' opacity='0.3'/%3E%3Crect x='24' y='12' width='14' height='8' rx='1' fill='%239c27b0' opacity='0.3'/%3E%3Crect x='10' y='22' width='28' height='10' rx='1' fill='%239c27b0' opacity='0.2'/%3E%3Crect x='18' y='36' width='12' height='4' rx='1' fill='white'/%3E%3C/svg%3E
content.content:
# User Interface Architecture

MeshWeaver generates UI where the data lives. Instead of transferring large datasets to clients, we compute visualizations server-side and stream only the rendered components. This dramatically reduces network traffic and enables real-time interactivity.

## The Data Compression Principle

Consider displaying a million-row dataset as a 10x10 summary table. Rather than transferring all rows to the browser:

<svg width="100%" height="200" viewBox="0 0 700 200" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <!-- Background -->
  <rect width="700" height="200" fill="#fafafa"/>
  
  <!-- Large Dataset (Left) -->
  <g transform="translate(50, 30)">
    <text x="60" y="0" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="12" fill="#666">Source Data</text>
    <!-- Grid representing many rows -->
    <rect x="0" y="10" width="120" height="140" fill="#e3f2fd" stroke="#1976d2" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- Row lines -->
    <line x1="0" y1="20" x2="120" y2="20" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="30" x2="120" y2="30" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="40" x2="120" y2="40" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="50" x2="120" y2="50" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="60" x2="120" y2="60" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <text x="60" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" fill="#1565c0">...</text>
    <line x1="0" y1="100" x2="120" y2="100" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="110" x2="120" y2="110" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="120" x2="120" y2="120" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="130" x2="120" y2="130" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="140" x2="120" y2="140" stroke="#90caf9" stroke-width="0.5"/>
    <!-- Label -->
    <text x="60" y="165" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="11" fill="#d32f2f" font-weight="bold">1,000,000 rows</text>
  </g>
  
  <!-- Arrow with Aggregate -->
  <g transform="translate(220, 80)">
    <rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="30" rx="15" fill="#4caf50"/>
    <text x="50" y="20" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="11" fill="white" font-weight="bold">Aggregate</text>
    <path d="M100 15 L130 15 L125 10 M130 15 L125 20" fill="none" stroke="#4caf50" stroke-width="2"/>
    <path d="M-30 15 L0 15" fill="none" stroke="#4caf50" stroke-width="2"/>
  </g>
  
  <!-- Resulting Table (Right) -->
  <g transform="translate(380, 40)">
    <text x="75" y="-10" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="12" fill="#666">Transferred Data</text>
    <!-- 10x10 grid -->
    <rect x="0" y="0" width="150" height="120" fill="#e8f5e9" stroke="#388e3c" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- Grid lines -->
    <g stroke="#a5d6a7" stroke-width="0.5">
      <line x1="15" y1="0" x2="15" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="30" y1="0" x2="30" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="45" y1="0" x2="45" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="60" y1="0" x2="60" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="75" y1="0" x2="75" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="90" y1="0" x2="90" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="105" y1="0" x2="105" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="120" y1="0" x2="120" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="135" y1="0" x2="135" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="12" x2="150" y2="12"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="24" x2="150" y2="24"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="36" x2="150" y2="36"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="48" x2="150" y2="48"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="60" x2="150" y2="60"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="72" x2="150" y2="72"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="84" x2="150" y2="84"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="96" x2="150" y2="96"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="108" x2="150" y2="108"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Label -->
    <text x="75" y="145" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="11" fill="#2e7d32" font-weight="bold">100 cells transferred</text>
  </g>
  
  <!-- Bandwidth comparison -->
  <g transform="translate(560, 60)">
    <rect x="0" y="0" width="120" height="80" rx="4" fill="#fff3e0" stroke="#f57c00" stroke-width="1"/>
    <text x="60" y="20" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" fill="#e65100" font-weight="bold">Bandwidth Saved</text>
    <text x="60" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="20" fill="#2e7d32" font-weight="bold">99.99%</text>
    <text x="60" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" fill="#666">Less data transfer</text>
  </g>
</svg>

## Controls Language

In MessageHubs, we define UI using a **Controls Language** - an immutable, declarative API that serializes to JSON:

```csharp
// Server-side control definition
Controls.Stack()
    .WithChildren(
        Controls.Text("Welcome!"),
        Controls.Button("Click Me").WithClickAction(OnClick),
        Controls.DataGrid(salesData)
    )
```

This serializes to JSON and streams to the browser for rendering.

## Two-Way Data Binding

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph Hub["MessageHub"]
        C[Controls Language]
        C --> J[JSON Serialization]
    end
    subgraph UI["Browser"]
        R[Renderer]
        V[View]
    end
    J -->|Stream| R
    R --> V
    V -->|Click Events| Hub
    V -->|Data Changes| Hub
```

**Key Features:**
- Controls defined server-side where data resides
- JSON serialization for transport
- Two-way binding: UI changes stream back to hubs
- Click events delivered as messages

## Control Lifecycle

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Hub
    participant Stream
    participant Browser
    Hub->>Stream: EntityStore (controls + data)
    Stream->>Browser: JSON / JSON Patch
    Browser->>Browser: Render Controls
    Browser->>Stream: ClickedEvent
    Stream->>Hub: Message Delivery
    Hub->>Hub: Execute ClickAction
```

### Incremental Updates

After initial load, only changes are transmitted using **JSON Patch** (RFC 6902):

```json
[{"op": "replace", "path": "/areas/counter/Data", "value": 42}]
```

This minimizes bandwidth for real-time updates.

## Available Controls

MeshWeaver provides a rich control library:

| Control | Purpose |
|---------|----------|
| `TextFieldControl` | Text input with validation |
| `SelectControl` | Dropdown selection |
| `DataGridControl` | Tabular data display |
| `ButtonControl` | Clickable actions |
| `DialogControl` | Modal dialogs |
| `EditFormControl` | Form containers |
| `LayoutAreaControl` | Nested layout regions |

## Interaction Handling

User interactions become messages:

```csharp
// Define a button with click handler
Controls.Button("Save")
    .WithClickAction(async context =>
    {
        // context.Area - which control was clicked
        // context.Payload - custom data
        // context.Hub - for posting messages
        await context.Hub.Post(new SaveRequest(data));
    })
```

When clicked, the browser sends a `ClickedEvent` message to the hub, which invokes the registered action.

## Benefits

1. **Bandwidth Efficiency**: Transfer summaries, not raw data
2. **Real-time Updates**: JSON Patch for incremental changes
3. **Security**: Data never leaves the server unnecessarily
4. **Consistency**: Single source of truth on server
5. **Flexibility**: Any control can be data-bound
